title: You asked for package — why did tests run?

mcq.question_md: A teammate runs `mvn package` to build the demo jar. The build fails on a broken unit test, and no jar appears in `target/`. Why did tests run when only `package` was asked for?
options: Maven has a bug — package should never touch tests
Lifecycle phases run in order, and test comes before package, so asking for package runs everything up to it
The teammate must have typed mvn test package by mistake
pom.xml had tests explicitly attached to the package phase
mcq.explanation_md: Maven's build lifecycle is a fixed sequence — `validate → compile → test → package → ...` — and requesting any phase runs **every phase before it**. So `mvn package` compiles, runs tests, and only packages if they pass. That's a feature: a jar built by Maven has, by definition, passed its tests. The tempting wrong answer is the pom-configuration one — no special wiring is needed; this ordering is built into Maven itself, identical in every project. If you truly must skip tests in an emergency, that's what `-DskipTests` is for — but the default protects you.
